title: Candidate-window second stage with focal-loss hard-negative mining
summary: A recent lap-level pit-stop paper reports severe class imbalance and uses focal loss.
rationale: A recent lap-level pit-stop paper reports severe class imbalance and uses focal loss. For this task, AUC is likely decided on ambiguous pre-stop laps, not on obvious early-stint negatives, so a second stage focused on hard windows may rank better than one global model.
implementation_hint: Keep the current broad model as stage 1, then train a stage 2 model only on plausible stop candidates or stage-1 hard negatives. Use focal loss or aggressive class weighting, and blend the refined score back only inside the candidate window.
expected_effect: Should improve ordering among near-stop laps without destabilizing the easy majority class, especially late in stints and around caution/weather transitions.
risk: A bad candidate filter can miss unusual early or opportunistic stops. Validate with grouped, purged folds rather than random CV.
